turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having a really tough time with my stomach lately. The cramps and diarrhea have just gotten worse over the past three weeks, and it's affecting my work and daily life, so I'm hoping we can figure out what's going on.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The main thing that's been bothering me is this crampy pain in my lower abdomen, mostly on the left side. It's like a dull ache that gets worse when I have to go to the bathroom, and then I get some bloody stool, which is really scary for me.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about three weeks ago, but looking back, I think it was maybe a little bit before that.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty frequently now - I'd say at least 8-10 times a day. Sometimes it's just a loose stool.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's about an 8 or 9 out of 10 - it's really debilitating and affects my whole quality of life.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: I also get this feeling of urgency, like I need to go right away, even if I've just gone.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Everything seems to make it worse - eating, drinking, even just being on my feet for too long. It's like my stomach is just constantly irritated and inflamed.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't really get any of those symptoms. The cramping and diarrhea are pretty much all I'm dealing with.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: My knees and ankles have been aching too, but I'm not sure if it's related to my stomach issues or something else.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, there's no swelling. The joint pain is just a dull ache, nothing like when I had a minor injury before.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking loperamide (Imodium) for about two weeks now to try and slow down my bowel movements, but it hasn't really made much of a difference.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any known medical conditions like that.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: Actually, I don't think I've ever been checked for anything like high cholesterol.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I'm a non-smoker, and I do drink beer occasionally.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: My diet is pretty irregular because of my long work hours. I usually end up eating takeout or grabbing something quick on the go. As for exercise, I try to stay active by going for walks during my lunch break and doing some light yoga at home.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mom is pretty healthy.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My family is pretty healthy on my mom's side.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over two years since I've seen a doctor.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I don't think so, at least not that I'm aware of.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been feeling a bit stressed lately due to work and these symptoms, but I'm trying to find ways to manage my stress.
